notes: Tests whether skill enforces SB7 canonical structure (hero + problem [external/internal/philosophical] + guide + plan + call-to-action + success + failure) vs baseline generic copywriting.

universal_assertions.text: Explicitly positions the customer as the hero and the brand/Isaac as the guide (not the hero)
Names or applies the three levels of problem: external, internal, philosophical
Positions the guide with both empathy and authority (competence)

evals.name: rp-one-liner
evals.prompt: Write me a one-liner for Rubinstein Productions. We help nonprofit EDs and mission-driven founders find their real voice so they can stop sounding like everyone else in their sector. We do this through facilitated conversations, brand video, and narrative work.

fixture_assertions.text: Follows the SB7 one-liner structure: character with a problem + guide with a plan + success/transformation
Names a specific transformation or success state, not vague 'grow your impact' language

evals.name: homepage-hero-copy
evals.prompt: Write hero-section copy for the Rubinstein Productions homepage. Target: nonprofit EDs who are tired of sector-generic messaging. We do Voice Liberation work — facilitation, narrative, film.

fixture_assertions.text: Surfaces both the external problem (generic messaging) and the internal problem (how it feels to sound like everyone else)
Includes a clear direct call-to-action, not a vague 'learn more'
